what: A change to running code — an edit at a seam in the implementation. Yields code; proved by exercising that code.
why: The default, most common node kind: work that compiles to a behavioral change. Distinct from skill (proved by cairnmap recompile) and concept-piece (proved by human review) — a code-seam is proved by the tester running a proof a hollow build couldn't pass. Named 'seam' not 'code' because the unit is the behavior/interface boundary the change lands at, where a proof can grip.
prove_gate: proof gate — the tester runs the node's proof and emits a VALIDATION; verdict and seal from the same hand. IOU: the tester isn't built, so proof is hand-run per the node's proofs/PROOF.md, culminating in a measured VALIDATION recorded by hand.
extra_gates: durability gate — a code-seam is not done until tested AND durably committed (git). Durability-of-stones sits on the emit chokepoint (physics, MAP.md 'Gates, not supervisors'), not a step anyone remembers to run: commit often (commit notes are notes to future selves), push at 'smells like done'. (Akien refinement 2026-07-15 — traces to the founding intention of getting Akien out of running the system.)
kicks_back_on: red proof (behavior doesn't match the falsifier) → routes back to design/build at the point of creation (CP2).
provenance: first code-seam, defined 2026-07-15 when the first running-code change was cast — discovered as demanded, not pre-seeded (the slate predicted this). Ratified by Akien (signature gate) 2026-07-15.
